description: Remove all Bun runtime code, Bun SQLite adapters, bun.lock, and update package.json scripts to use npm/Node.js only. Use 'tsx' instead of '--experimental-strip-types' for stable TypeScript execution.
steps: Add 'tsx' as a production dependency in package.json (it is needed at runtime for start.ts and ensure-middleware-manifest.ts). Run: npm install tsx

Update package.json scripts: dev to 'next dev --hostname 127.0.0.1 --turbopack --port 12712', build to 'NODE_ENV=production next build --turbopack && tsx ./scripts/ensure-middleware-manifest.ts', start to 'NODE_ENV=production tsx ./scripts/start.ts', test to 'vitest run --reporter=verbose'

Rewrite bin/axonrouter.js: Remove 'const isBun = typeof globalThis.Bun !== "undefined";'. Remove the Bun-related condition in hasStripTypes. Remove the entire catch block that spawns bun as fallback. The re-spawn logic should now use tsx instead of --experimental-strip-types. The logic should be: check if running under tsx (check for tsx in execArgv or process.env._), if not, re-spawn with tsx (spawn 'npx' with args ['tsx', ...process.argv.slice(1)]). Or simpler: use a shebang approach where the bin just does: import {main} from '../scripts/start.ts' after spawning via tsx. Simplest approach: the bin file should detect if TS is supported (try importing .ts), if not re-spawn with tsx. Keep it simple and stable.

In src/lib/sqliteHelpers.ts: Remove isBunRuntime() function, remove BunSQLiteStatement class, remove BunSQLiteAdapter class, remove BunSQLiteDatabase function. Simplify loadDatabaseDriver() to always return NodeSQLiteDatabase. Keep NodeSQLiteDatabase and the BetterSqliteDatabase import.

In src/lib/requestDetailsDb/core.ts: Remove BunLike type, remove 'const BunRuntime = ...' line, remove BunSQLiteStatement class, remove BunSQLiteAdapter class, remove BunSQLiteDatabase function. Simplify loadDatabaseDriver() to always return NodeSQLiteDatabase.

In src/lib/usageDb/core.ts: Remove BunDatabaseCtor type, remove BunSQLiteStatement class, remove BunSQLiteAdapter class, remove 'const BunSQLiteDatabase: DatabaseDriver = BunSQLiteAdapter;'. Simplify loadDatabaseDriver() to always return NodeSQLiteDatabase.

In src/lib/sqliteWriteGate.ts: Update the JSDoc comment to remove Bun-specific explanation. Keep the write gate logic as-is (it is still useful for serializing writes regardless of runtime). Change the comment to explain it prevents concurrent write conflicts in synchronous SQLite operations.

In tests/unit/start-redis-default.test.ts: Update the test description from 'resolves the local Next CLI for direct node/bun start execution' to 'resolves the local Next CLI for direct node start execution'
acceptance_criteria: package.json scripts contain no 'bun' references and use 'tsx' for .ts execution
bin/axonrouter.js contains no Bun detection or bun spawn fallback, uses tsx for TS support
src/lib/sqliteHelpers.ts has no isBunRuntime, BunSQLiteStatement, BunSQLiteAdapter, or BunSQLiteDatabase
src/lib/requestDetailsDb/core.ts has no BunLike, BunRuntime, BunSQLiteStatement, BunSQLiteAdapter, or BunSQLiteDatabase
src/lib/usageDb/core.ts has no BunDatabaseCtor, BunSQLiteStatement, BunSQLiteAdapter, or BunSQLiteDatabase
npm run typecheck passes (no type errors introduced)
findings: Pre-existing typecheck failures (5 errors in CSS imports and JSX style tags) and 3 pre-existing test failures (modernization-inventory, provider-icon-assets-smoke, start-redis-default path test) all confirmed on base state. The test script needed --root tests flag since the old script used bun --cwd tests. tsx added as prod dependency for stable TS execution without --experimental-strip-types.
